meta.rubric: 5-dimension equal-weighted mean (modernness, attractiveness, coherence, ui_clarity, accessibility), rounded to nearest 0.5
meta.tooling_note: WAVE/axe could not be run inside this environment; Accessibility scored conservatively from observable markup (alt-text presence, contrast ratios computed from sampled hex, focus-style declarations). AETOS returned HTTP 403 on root and sub-pages — scored from observable/public knowledge per methodology §7.3. Screenshots not captured (no browser automation in this run).

competitors.name: Certis
competitors.website_design_notes: Hero 'Redefining the future of operations' is a brand line, not a what-we-sell line; service unclear until 'Read More'. No pricing; alt-text absent on markup images.
competitors.mobile_pass: Strong. Large-format hero photography and section cards reflow cleanly; nav collapses to hamburger. Same brand-abstraction problem on both viewports.

competitors.name: Aspectus
competitors.website_design_notes: Visual fragmentation across Aspectus/Lexasure/Archipelago sub-brands. Strongest coherence in set — hero names the manpower+tech model directly. Alt-text not present on markup.
competitors.mobile_pass: Adequate. 'Try the Demo' CTA carried into mobile nav. Multi-brand logo soup feels more cramped at 375px.

competitors.name: AETOS
competitors.website_design_notes: Site returned HTTP 403 — blocked automated audit. Scored conservatively from public knowledge: established institutional security brand, conventional corporate-CMS presentation; no observable pricing.
competitors.mobile_pass: Not assessable — site blocked fetch on every attempted URL.

competitors.name: SECOM Singapore
competitors.website_design_notes: Carousel hero, generic flat icons, traditional footer, no whitespace asymmetry — reads 2-3 years stale. 'LEARN MORE' teal-on-white CTA contrast marginal; alt-text absent.
competitors.mobile_pass: Weak. Carousel collapses to a static stack; 'Leader in Loss Prevention' headline reads as an unscannable slogan with no service anchor.

competitors.name: Ademco Security Group
competitors.website_design_notes: Repetitive hero blocks, static grids, no micro-interactions — 2019-2021 era. #C42632 red on white passes AA but alt-text missing on partner/logo images.
competitors.mobile_pass: Adequate. Stock-photo hero and solution grid reflow predictably; 'Enterprise Security at your fingertips' scans fine at 375px.

competitors.name: ISS Facility Services Singapore
competitors.website_design_notes: Hero 'Creating value for your company' is generic and does not state the FM service; multi-level menus add decode load. Stock AdobeStock hero, inconsistent alt-text.
competitors.mobile_pass: Adequate. Global ISS template reflows competently; deep menus become a long hamburger accordion.

competitors.name: CBM Pte Ltd
competitors.website_design_notes: Squarespace-template base, no modern button treatments; a 2021 CNY banner still in hero rotation signals stale maintenance. Alt-text absent, no focus styling.
competitors.mobile_pass: Adequate-but-dated. Squarespace responsive defaults handle reflow; the stale CNY-2021 banner undermines credibility on both viewports.

competitors.name: Oneberry
competitors.website_design_notes: Legacy WordPress + Revolution Slider with unloaded placeholder hero images; 11-item Solutions mega-menu adds decode load; alt-text and focus states absent. Best proof storytelling in the set — named case studies, testimonial quotes, government-grant eligibility spelled out.
competitors.mobile_pass: Weak. Heavy slider hero and an over-long Solutions hamburger list; closest direct analogue to Elitez (ARVAS video analytics) yet the weakest-presenting site in the set.

competitors.name: Soverus
competitors.website_design_notes: Site repeatedly timed out on load (60s+) — a severe availability failure that blocks prospects outright. ICC-first positioning and SGX-listed parent (Secura Group) give credibility, but a site a buyer cannot reach scores at the bottom of the field.
competitors.mobile_pass: Not assessable — the site timed out on every load attempt.

competitors.name: Secura Group
competitors.website_design_notes: Clean, responsive layout and a broad named service grid; SGX Catalist-listed with an 800+ customer claim. But the services-page hero 'Our Security Solutions' is flat, sections repeat, and there are no case studies, client logos, certification badges, pricing, demo or ROI tool.
competitors.mobile_pass: Adequate. Responsive grid reflows cleanly; the flat hero and undifferentiated service tiles read the same at 375px.

self_audit.subject: Elitez Integrated Command Center
self_audit.notes: Accessibility 6: styles.css declares 'outline:none' with no :focus-visible replacement — keyboard focus likely invisible (WCAG 2.4.7 risk). Otherwise: all 7 in-body photos carry descriptive alt-text; hero text #14233b on #eef1f5 ~12:1, white-on-#00337a CTA ~10.8:1, steel #5a6b85 on white ~4.9:1 (AA pass). Strongest coherence + attractiveness in the field.
self_audit.mobile_pass: Good. Tailwind grid with explicit lg: breakpoints; hero h1 steps 3.7→3.4→2.6rem so it stays scannable. Nav collapses to a labelled hamburger drawer surfacing every item including ROI Calculator. No dimension degrades >1 point on mobile.

easy_wins.title: Restore a visible focus ring
easy_wins.detail: styles.css ships 'outline:none'. Add a high-contrast :focus-visible ring (2px solid var(--blue), 2px offset) on all links, buttons and the nav toggle. Closes the one real Accessibility defect and lifts that dimension 6→8.

easy_wins.title: Publish an indicative price band on the ROI Calculator page
easy_wins.detail: Elitez already wins findability (pricing 4s) but every competitor scored pricing=null. A real 'from S$X/site/month' or tier table on roi-calculator.html converts a tie into a category-unique transparency advantage.

easy_wins.title: Run WAVE/axe and fix any contrast alerts on steel text
easy_wins.detail: Steel #5a6b85 on white is ~4.9:1 — an AA pass but borderline; on #eef1f5 paper it drops below 4.5:1. Darken steel to ~#4a5a73 where it sits on paper.

easy_wins.title: Add a skip-to-content link and confirm landmark semantics
easy_wins.detail: The page uses <main> already; add a visually-hidden skip link and verify header/nav/footer landmarks so the site clears the structural axe rules the rest of the category fails.

easy_wins.title: Tighten the dual-CTA hierarchy in the hero
easy_wins.detail: Hero shows 'Explore Services' + 'Calculate Your Savings' plus 'Request a Walkthrough' in nav — three live CTAs. Keep the primary/secondary weighting unambiguous so buyer intent is never split.

ten_x_aspirations.title: A live operations-floor motif as the brand's distinctive layer
ten_x_aspirations.detail: Modernness caps at 7 because the site is clean but template-adjacent (Tailwind CDN, no signature motion). Build one restrained system — a faint live 'watch ticker' in the utility bar, or a data-grid backdrop that animates once on load — so the site reads as its own command-centre brand. Target Modernness 9.

ten_x_aspirations.title: An interactive embedded command-centre demo instead of a mailto walkthrough
ten_x_aspirations.detail: Every competitor including Elitez scores demo via 'contact us'. A self-serve mini-demo — a clickable mock video-wall flagging an event and firing a dispatch — would make Elitez the only site where a prospect experiences the product before talking to sales.

ten_x_aspirations.title: Be the WCAG-AA reference site in a category that fails accessibility
ten_x_aspirations.detail: Not one competitor cleared 6 on Accessibility. A genuine clean axe run (focus rings, skip link, alt on every asset, 200% reflow verified) lets Elitez claim an accessibility/compliance posture that regulator-facing buyers visibly value.

ten_x_aspirations.title: A transparent pricing/ROI page as a category wedge
ten_x_aspirations.detail: Pricing=null across all eight competitors is the biggest shared weakness. A fully public ROI calculator outputting a real monthly figure and a manpower-cost comparison turns Elitez's ROI tool into the category's only honest buying surface.

category_summary: The SG security / integrated-FM category presents online as institutionally competent but commercially opaque and visually behind. Pricing is invisible everywhere (findability null for all 10 competitors). Accessibility is universally weak (none above 6 — alt-text gaps and missing focus states are universal; AETOS 403s automated visitors, Soverus times out entirely). Hero copy hides the service behind brand abstraction at Certis, SECOM and ISS. Elitez self-audits at 7.5, above every competitor (next best Aspectus 6.5; field median ~5.5), winning decisively on Coherence (9) and Attractiveness (8). Its one real defect — an invisible focus ring — is a trivial fix that, combined with a public price band, would convert two category-wide blind spots into Elitez advantages.

service_gaps.evidence: ISS markets catering + M&E + a branded Pure Space cleaning family; CBM runs 11 divisions incl. engineering, car-park, laundry.
service_gaps.action: Name M&E maintenance, cleaning, landscaping and pest control as explicit IFM lines — not a vague bundle.

service_gaps.evidence: Oneberry (Invisiron), Soverus and Secura (post-Onesecure) all sell cyber alongside physical monitoring.
service_gaps.action: Add a network / cyber-defence attachment — natural bundle for a business selling AI analytics over networks.

service_gaps.evidence: Aspectus sells security audits, risk assessments and red-teaming; Secura sells risk advisory and TSCM.
service_gaps.action: Offer a low-cost site-security audit as a frictionless wedge into a monitoring contract.

service_gaps.gap: FM-incident handling inside the command centre
service_gaps.evidence: AETOS's ICC already triages lift breakdowns and water leaks as detected events; Certis fuses FM into one operations centre.
service_gaps.action: Build and advertise FM-fault triage in the ICC — the most credible proof of the CCaaS-into-IFM thesis.

service_gaps.evidence: Secura runs a SkillsFuture-approved WSQ academy — revenue plus a recruiting moat.
service_gaps.action: Roadmap a 'command-centre operator' certification track; fits the SG-government register. Lower priority.

capability_gaps.evidence: Ademco displays ISO 22301 / ISO 27001 / SS558 / SPF CAMS / SCDF DECAMS; SECOM is CAMS-licensed. Elitez shows none.
capability_gaps.action: Surface every licence and standard already held — a copy/badge change, decisive for regulator-facing buyers.

capability_gaps.evidence: Ademco '8,000 institutions', SECOM '2,000+ customers', Secura '800+ customers', ISS '50,000+ sites'.
capability_gaps.action: State an honest officer / site / camera / stream count to anchor credibility.

capability_gaps.evidence: Ademco's VERIFSUITE (Video / Ops / Enterprise / Robo) is the field's only real SKU ladder.
capability_gaps.action: Put the four pricing tiers (Sentinel / Garrison / Command Partner / Group Cost-Allocation) on the public site.

capability_gaps.evidence: Certis (humanoid + autonomous robots), AETOS (drones, 5G control), Aspectus, Oneberry, Soverus all advertise physical automation.
capability_gaps.action: Advertise partner-sourced robot integration — no capex needed, but stop being the only one silent on it.

capability_gaps.evidence: Oneberry spells out 80% robot / 50% cyber grant eligibility as a buying incentive.
capability_gaps.action: Put the EDG / PSG / WDG-JR+ / CCP grant pathways on the public site as a 'what you'll actually pay' panel.

website_adopt.detail: Named case studies with hard numbers (Sentosa 200+ cameras), testimonial quotes, a sharp market stat ('98% of CCTV footage isn't watched').
Visible ISO/SS certification badges, a partner-brand logo grid, and a scale claim above the fold.
Names the manpower+technology model directly; the only competitor with a working 'Try the Demo' CTA.
FM outlook reports give the site a reason-to-return and SEO depth — no security competitor does this.

whitespace: Transparent, productised pricing — zero of 10 competitors publish any price.
An interactive, experienceable command-centre demo — nobody lets a prospect see the product work before contacting sales.
Command centre + IFM at an SME / dormitory price point — the integrators (Certis, AETOS) price it out; the agencies and FM players each hold only one half.
WCAG-AA accessibility as a published compliance posture — every competitor site fails accessibility; two block visitors entirely.
A content voice specific to command-centre-as-a-service — the category has no educator.
